solution.description: Foundation configuration, mapping, policy, runtime projection, and operation schema for Planora / CCSB.

tables.description: Operational location, facility, service area, region, cluster, or virtual location used to scope boards, resources, services, availability, and permissions.

fields.description: Stable human-readable or source-system code for the location.
Classifies how the location is used operationally.
Stable key used to reconcile this location to an external source.
IANA or Windows time-zone identifier used for local schedule display and validation.
Primary street address line.
Secondary address line.
Suburb, city, or locality.
State, province, or territory.
Postal or ZIP code.
ISO country code.
Latitude for travel, proximity, or mapping calculations.
Longitude for travel, proximity, or mapping calculations.
Default capacity available at this location when a service does not define an override.
Date from which the location may be used.
Date after which the location may not be used.
Display order within board navigation and grouping.
Indicates whether new scheduling activity may be allocated to this location.
Operational notes about the location.

tables.description: Stable product-level board registry identity. A board registry owns its configuration lifecycle and points to one active immutable Board Version.

fields.description: Stable unique code used in integrations, URLs, and configuration references.
Business purpose and operating scope of the board.
Primary operational use of the board.
Business lifecycle of the board identity.
Default IANA or Windows time-zone identifier for board rendering.
Default number of days loaded when the board opens.
Maximum configured group hierarchy depth for this board.
Allows authorised planners to create or amend schedule records outside configured templates.
Allows valid schedule items to remain intentionally unassigned.
Stable external identifier for the board.
Date from which the board may be used.
Date after which the board may not be used.

tables.description: Immutable, versioned configuration baseline governing mappings, views, rules, groups, status models, role definitions, and permission policies for a Board.

fields.description: Monotonically increasing version number within the Board.
Readable release label, for example 2026.1 or Winter Release.
CCSB product semantic version that last validated or activated this board version.
Configuration schema version used by this normalized board version.
Compatibility migration state for this board version.
Latest product and schema compatibility status used by validation and activation gates.
Lifecycle of the immutable board configuration version.
Latest validation outcome for the version.
Timestamp from which the version is permitted to be active.
Timestamp after which the version must not be active.
Timestamp at which the version became active.
Deterministic hash of normalized configuration content used to identify drift.
Marks the version as immutable after activation or publication.
Reason and summary for this version.
Timestamp of the most recent validation run.
Stable external identifier for the board version.

tables.description: Approved definition of a native or customer-owned Dataverse table that participates in a Board Version.

fields.description: Dataverse logical name of the mapped source table.
Dataverse Web API entity-set name.
Semantic scheduling role fulfilled by the source table.
Whether the table is native CCSB or customer-owned.
Logical name of the source primary ID field.
Logical name of the primary display name field.
Safe default sort expression for runtime retrieval.
Approved OData filter applied when this entity is queried by the board.
Approved FetchXML filter applied where OData is not sufficient.
Permits the runtime to read records from the mapped source.
Permits the runtime to create records in the mapped source.
Permits the runtime to update approved mapped fields.
Permits the runtime to delete records in the mapped source.
Allows the runtime to open the mapped Dataverse form for a record.
Metadata fingerprint used to detect source schema drift.
Timestamp at which source metadata was last validated.
Current metadata validation outcome.
Indicates whether this entity definition is active for its Board Version.

tables.description: Maps an approved source field to a CCSB scheduling semantic and defines controlled transformation and write behavior.

fields.description: Stable CCSB semantic, for example activity.start or assignment.resource.
Logical name of the mapped source field.
Dataverse metadata type of the mapped source field.
Expected semantic data type after transformation.
Transformation applied before a value is consumed or written.
Controlled expression or JSON definition of the transformation.
Explicit mapping of source choice values to CCSB semantic values.
Value used when the source value is blank or unavailable.
Expected target table when the source field is a lookup.
Display field used when resolving a lookup.
Requires the mapping to resolve successfully before activation.
Allows the semantic value to be null.
Allows this mapping to be used in runtime reads.
Permitted runtime write behavior for this mapping.
Optional validation expression or regular expression.
Evaluation order where fields depend on other mappings.
Indicates whether the field mapping is active.

tables.description: Maps approved Dataverse relationships to CCSB scheduling graph semantics.

fields.description: Stable semantic such as booking.activity or activity.assignment.
Dataverse relationship schema/logical name.
Cardinality of the source relationship.
Source lookup field used to resolve the relationship.
Web API navigation property from the source record.
Web API navigation or collection property from the target record.
Primary identifier field of the related source table.
Requires the relationship to resolve before activation.
Permitted runtime write behavior for this relationship.
Intended business delete policy. Physical lookup relationships are created as Restrict.
Optional condition limiting related records used by the runtime.
Evaluation order.
Indicates whether the relationship mapping is active.

tables.description: Reusable lifecycle model governing allowed status states, transitions, display colors, and runtime status-write policy.

fields.description: Stable code for the status model.
Scheduling semantic governed by this status model.
System permitted to own the lifecycle status.
Initial status key used when a record is created.
JSON array of terminal status keys.
JSON color and display configuration for statuses.
Allows mapped external records to update status through approved mappings.
Allows authorised users to override transition validation.
How invalid transitions are handled.
Indicates whether the status model is active.
Display and evaluation order.

tables.description: Configuration-driven named resource role used by activity requirements and assignments, including quantity and capacity rules for group bookings.

fields.description: Stable resource role code.
Business definition and usage notes.
High-level category of resource role.
Default number of equivalent resources required.
Minimum number of resources required to satisfy the role.
Maximum number of resources permitted for the role.
Default required capacity contribution for this role.
Unit for capacity values, for example seats, hours, or kilograms.
Approved configuration filter identifying compatible resources.
Configured skill, qualification, or certification requirements.
Allows multiple equivalent resources to satisfy the role.
Allows a compatible resource to substitute for a preferred type.
Allows this role to be assigned concurrently where other rules permit it.
Hex color used for role display where configured.
Display order.
Indicates whether the resource role is active.

tables.description: Product-level schedule board permission policy. Dataverse security roles remain the enforcement mechanism; this profile expresses authorised board actions and scoped access.

fields.description: Stable permission-profile code.
Purpose and authorised operational scope.
Intended operational persona.
Scope used when evaluating permission assignments.
Allows viewing board data in scope.
Allows creating schedule records in scope.
Allows editing schedule records in scope.
Allows assigning or unassigning resources.
Allows an authorised override of warnings or conflicts.
Allows publishing approved schedule versions.
Allows rollback of a publish operation where lifecycle policy permits it.
Allows draft configuration changes.
Allows viewing immutable snapshots and operation evidence.
Allows controlled support actions for integration operations.
Optional supporting Dataverse security-role name used by deployment validation.
Indicates whether the permission profile is active.

tables.description: Binds a Permission Profile to a Dataverse principal and optional board, location, or group scope.

fields.description: Type of Dataverse or directory principal receiving the profile.
Dataverse or directory principal identifier stored as a string for polymorphic portability.
Friendly principal name captured for administration and audit.
Whether the assignment grants or explicitly denies the profile.
Scope to which the permission assignment applies.
Polymorphic scope record identifier stored as text.
Friendly scope name retained for administration and audit.
Timestamp from which the assignment is effective.
Timestamp after which the assignment is no longer effective.
Optional approval ticket or change reference.
Indicates whether the assignment is active.

tables.description: Read-only materialized runtime projection of approved normalized CCSB configuration. It is never the configuration source of truth.

fields.description: Kind of runtime projection.
Scope represented by the projection.
Stable scope key used to retrieve the projection.
Read-only serialized projection payload.
Indicates whether the payload is compressed or encoded.
Hash of the projection payload.
Version of the runtime projection contract.
CCSB product semantic version used to generate this projection.
Configuration schema version of the normalized source used for this projection.
Source configuration change token used for staleness detection.
Timestamp at which the projection was generated.
Optional projection expiry timestamp.
Current usability of the projection.
Technical error detail when projection creation fails.
Number of logical records represented by the projection.
Size of the serialized payload.
Indicates whether this is the current projection for its type and scope.

tables.description: Evidence record produced by validation of a Board Version, mapping, policy, or runtime projection.

fields.description: Correlation identifier shared by validation results produced in one run.
Stable validation rule code.
Category of validation performed.
Severity of the validation outcome.
Current handling state of the validation result.
Logical name of the affected entity.
Affected record identifier stored as text.
Logical name of the affected field.
Short validation message for end users and administrators.
Machine-readable evidence and detailed diagnostic content.
Suggested corrective action.
Timestamp at which the issue was detected.
Timestamp at which the issue was resolved or accepted.
Reason for resolution, acceptance, or suppression.
Source-system or technical error code where applicable.

tables.description: Auditable asynchronous or synchronous product operation, including validation, projection generation, publish, rollback, import, and controlled lifecycle actions.

fields.description: Type of operation executed by the product.
Current execution status.
End-to-end correlation identifier for telemetry and support.
Stable request key preventing duplicate operation processing.
Scope acted on by the operation.
Polymorphic target identifier stored as text.
Timestamp at which the operation was requested.
Timestamp at which processing began.
Timestamp at which processing ended.
Validated input payload supplied to the operation.
Result or summary payload produced by the operation.
Short error summary for user-facing operation diagnostics.
Detailed technical error evidence.
Number of processing attempts.
Earliest timestamp at which a retry may be attempted.
Total operation items expected.
Operation items successfully processed.
Operation items that failed.
Reported progress from 0 to 100.
Indicates whether the operation may be cancelled safely.
Date after which operational evidence may be archived according to policy.

tables.description: Child-level processing and audit evidence for one item handled by a CCSB Operation.

fields.description: Deterministic sequence within the parent operation.
Stable per-operation item key.
Type of item processed.
Action attempted for the operation item.
Current result for the operation item.
Logical name of the source record.
Source record identifier stored as text.
Logical name of the target record.
Target record identifier stored as text.
Item-level input payload.
Item-level result payload.
Technical or business error code.
Short item-level error message.
Detailed technical error content.
Timestamp at which this item was last processed.
Number of retries attempted for this item.
Indicates whether this item is safe to retry.
Processing duration for the item.
End-to-end correlation identifier.

Board version governing this resource role.

Configuration version used to build the projection.

Stable product board registry identity governing this operational schedule board.

Configuration version governing this published schedule version.

Version governing the event type definition.
Version governing the resource definition.
Version governing the group definition.

fields.description: Stable external identifier for resource reconciliation.
Date from which the resource is eligible for the board.
Date after which the resource is not eligible for the board.
Stable external identifier for service reconciliation.
Indicates whether a location must be resolved before scheduling.
Operational time zone used for schedule calculations and display.
Controls whether this board may load and accept scheduling operations.
Stable external identifier for schedule-item reconciliation.

notes: ccsb_boardregistry is the stable configuration identity. ccsb_scheduleboard remains the operational schedule-board record and may resolve to ccsb_board in Core environments.
ccsb_boardversion is the immutable configuration version. ccsb_scheduleversion remains the operational/published schedule version.

All extension lookup columns are optional at metadata level so existing V1 records can be migrated without import failure. Publish and activation logic must validate the business-required relationships.
ccsb_runtimeprojection is a read-only materialized projection and must never replace the normalized configuration source of truth.

ccsb_permissionprofile and ccsb_permissionassignment express product scope. Dataverse security roles, team membership, business-unit access, and Custom API authorization enforce the actual permissions.

coreEntityAliases.description: Resolves the existing V1 Schedule Board table by exact logical name first, then by its Dataverse display name. This protects the foundation schema from an unverified logical-name assumption in older Core builds.
